title: Mnesis Embodiment Registry
description: Schema for embodiment registry entries: one JSON file per embodiment. Authority: Parthenon D-8 / S12. Each file declares the canonical identity, kinematics, asset paths, and teleop defaults for one robot or human embodiment.

id.description: Embodiment identifier, used as filename stem (e.g. 'airbot_play').

dof_per_arm.description: Number of degrees of freedom per arm, excluding gripper (e.g. 6 for a 6-DOF arm).

joint_names.description: Joint names in order. For dual-arm embodiments, concatenate left then right joints (e.g. [left_j1, ..., left_jN, right_j1, ..., right_jN]).

min.description: Minimum joint positions in radians, one per joint_name entry.

max.description: Maximum joint positions in radians, one per joint_name entry.

joint_limits.description: Position limits for each joint in radians. Arrays must match joint_names length.

urdf.description: Path to URDF file, or empty string if not available.

mjcf.description: Path to MuJoCo MJCF file, or empty string if not available.

glb.description: Path to GLB (glTF Binary) file, or empty string if not available.

skeleton.description: Path to skeleton/keypoints file, or empty string if not available.

assets.description: Asset paths for rendering, simulation, and skeleton data.

default_pos_scale.description: Default position scaling factor for teleoperation (e.g. 0.3 for fine control).

workspace_box.description: Workspace bounding box [[x_min, y_min, z_min], [x_max, y_max, z_max]] in metres.

capture.description: Optional (additive, v0.5+) capture-side defaults so a device can be reconfigured by embodiment id instead of hard-coding capture parameters per consumer. Absence = consumer keeps its own defaults.

default_fps.description: Default recording frame rate in frames per second.

max_duration_s.description: Recommended per-episode duration cap in seconds.

cameras.description: Camera set and resolution for this embodiment's default rig.

fps.description: Per-camera frame rate if it differs from default_fps (optional).

gripper_capture.description: How the gripper channel is sampled during capture.

mode.description: 'continuous' = analog stroke, 'binary' = open/close switch, 'none' = no gripper.

normalized_range.description: Normalized capture range, conventionally [0.0, 1.0] (0 = open, 1 = closed). Physical stroke lives in gripper_range.

demonstration_modes.description: Teaching modes this embodiment supports; consumers switch capture UI accordingly.

calibration.description: Calibration requirements for a valid capture session.

hand_eye_required.description: Whether hand-eye (camera↔arm extrinsic) calibration is required before capture.

capture_profiles.description: Optional (additive, v0.5+) named capture presets. A registry entry may carry several; a consumer selects one by name to configure a recording session (task, frame rate, camera subset, annotation template).

name.description: Preset identifier, unique within this embodiment (e.g. 'tabletop_pick_place_v1').

task.description: Human-readable task this preset targets (e.g. 'tabletop pick-and-place').

fps.description: Recording frame rate for this preset.

cameras.description: Camera keys used by this preset (subset of capture.cameras names).
